decisions.title: Keep architecture state in project-local JSON
decisions.context: ClaimsDesk needs architecture documentation that both humans and LLMs can use without a hosted documentation system.
decisions.decision: Store architecture state as strict JSON files under docs/architext/data and render a local read-only site from those files.
consequences: Validation becomes part of the development workflow

decisions.title: Split browser app from Claims API
decisions.context: The product needs fast UI iteration while keeping sensitive persistence and authorization on the server.
decisions.decision: Use a static React web app backed by a separately deployed Claims API.
consequences: API must enforce all authorization server-side

decisions.title: Run fraud review asynchronously
decisions.context: Fraud scoring can be slow and depends on an external vendor.
decisions.decision: Publish fraud review jobs to a queue and process them with a worker.
consequences: The system must handle queued and manual review states

decisions.title: Store claim state relationally
decisions.context: Claim state transitions need transactional guarantees and staff query support.
decisions.decision: Use PostgreSQL for claim records, review state, and payout state.
consequences: Document blobs remain outside the relational database

decisions.title: Store uploaded documents in object storage
decisions.context: Claim evidence files can be large and should not pass through the API process.
decisions.decision: Use private object storage with short-lived signed upload URLs.
consequences: Signed URL leakage becomes a first-class risk

decisions.title: Use managed identity provider
decisions.context: ClaimsDesk needs OIDC, role claims, and account recovery without owning credential storage.
decisions.decision: Use Auth0 for authentication and token issuance.
consequences: Auth0 outage affects login and token refresh

decisions.title: Record security-relevant events in append-only audit log
decisions.context: Staff approvals and payout changes need compliance visibility.
decisions.decision: Write approval and automated review events to an append-only audit log.
consequences: Compliance export has a single source
Tamper evidence must be strengthened before regulated launch

decisions.title: Use external payment gateway for payouts
decisions.context: ClaimsDesk should not directly integrate with banking rails in the first release.
decisions.decision: Send approved payout instructions to a payment gateway.
consequences: Webhook authenticity and reconciliation become critical

decisions.title: De-identify analytics data before warehouse load
decisions.context: Analytics users need operational insight without broad access to claimant PII.
decisions.decision: Strip direct identifiers before loading claims and audit metrics into the warehouse.
consequences: Re-identification risk still needs periodic review
